ques: Retrieve a short biography of LeBron James.
answer: LeBron Raymone James (born December 30, 1984, in Akron, Ohio) is an American professional basketball player for the Los Angeles Lakers of the NBA. Nicknamed "King James," he went prep-to-pro from St. Vincent–St. Mary High School and was selected first overall by the Cleveland Cavaliers in the 2003 NBA draft, winning Rookie of the Year. He has won four NBA championships (2012, 2013 with Miami; 2016 with Cleveland; 2020 with the Lakers) and four Finals MVP awards across three teams, along with four NBA MVP awards and three Olympic gold medals. He is the NBA's all-time leading scorer, a record 22-time All-Star, and is widely considered one of the greatest basketball players of all time.
